title: Fabric spray starch
subtitle: A cornflour spray starch for cotton and linen that gives body and a crisp finish when ironing
excerpt: Commercial spray starch is cornflour or rice starch in water with propellants and preservatives. This version does the same job without the aerosol. A light spray on cotton or linen before ironing gives the fabric body and a crisp, smooth finish. Shake before each use.
sourceNotes: Cornflour spray starch method from historical laundry references and modern natural cleaning guides.

recipe.batchNotes: Make in larger quantities. Store in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Cool before transferring to spray bottle.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 2 weeks refrigerated. The cornflour is a food product and will grow mould if left at room temperature beyond a day or two. Make in small batches and refrigerate. Discard if the liquid develops any odour or cloudiness that does not shake clear.

recipeTools.notes: Must have a fine mist setting

glossaryTerms.definition: A fine starch extracted from maize (corn). When dissolved in water and heated, it gelatinises into a clear paste. In fabric starch, this paste coats the fibres and stiffens the fabric when dry, giving a crisp ironing finish.

The process by which starch granules absorb water and swell when heated, breaking open and thickening the liquid into a translucent paste. The gelatinised starch is the active component in spray starch.

The technique of applying a fine mist of starch solution to fabric while ironing. The iron's heat dries the starch and sets the fibres in position, producing a smooth, crisp finish. Use sparingly; too much causes flaking.

content.text: when heated in water, producing a clear paste that stiffens fabric fibres when dried by an iron. This

method is effective on cotton and linen shirts, collars, and tablecloths. Apply to slightly damp fabric. One tablespoon of cornflour in 250 ml water gives a light starch; increase to 2 tablespoons for a crisper finish. Shelf life is 2 weeks refrigerated.

items.prepNote: Approximately 8 g; increase to 2 tablespoons for heavier starch

content.text: Dissolve the cornflour in the cold water in a small saucepan, whisking to remove lumps. Add the remaining water and heat over a medium flame, stirring constantly, until the mixture comes to a gentle simmer and thickens slightly to a clear, slightly viscous liquid. Remove from heat and allow to cool completely.
Transfer to the spray bottle. Shake before use. Spray a light mist onto the fabric from a distance of 20 to 30 cm before ironing.
Refrigerate between uses. Shelf life is 2 weeks. Shake before each use as the starch settles. Discard if any odour or mould appears. Make in small batches to minimise waste.
